title: matelea sanojana
intro: Matelea sanojana is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a woody climber with medicinal properties, traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, and also provide health benefits including wound healing and antiseptic effects. In herbal preparations, Matelea sanojana is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
